differential_diagnoses: Primary angiitis of the central nervous system (PACNS)
Neurodegenerative dementia (e.g., Alzheimer’s disease with CAA)
Metastatic malignancy to the temporal lobes
next_steps: Send CSF for autoimmune encephalitis panel (including anti-LGI1, anti-NMDA, anti-Hu/Yo/Ri)
Contrast-enhanced CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis to screen for occult malignancy
Initiate pulse-dose intravenous methylprednisolone for suspected inflammatory/autoimmune etiology
Obtain MRI of the brain with specialized sequences to evaluate for leptomeningeal enhancement or FLAIR-hyperintense lesions
Consider brain biopsy if clinical status declines despite empirical immunotherapy or if malignancy is strongly suspected
Maintain antiseizure therapy (Lacosamide or Levetiracetam) due to EEG findings
